113853.explanation: That is the role of the municipal council. It is also not practical and delays the process. Our faction speaks frequently with residents and visits many associations and community centers.

113854.explanation: This is already being done. It is existing policy.

113857.explanation: The consequences cannot be foreseen. Local generation must be profitable so that a structural contribution from the municipality is not necessary.

113860.explanation: We do not encourage it, but we do take responsibility.

113861.explanation: Everyone deserves a dignified place to live. We also do not want cluttering in the countryside.

113865.explanation: We wonder whether there is a need for this and if it is even a task for the municipality.

113868.explanation: This is the task of the entrepreneur.

113869.explanation: The rules for plot subdivision have just been relaxed. This should offer sufficient possibilities.

113871.explanation: We have already arranged this through our own amendment.

113873.explanation: We do want to provide practical support and advice. For the accommodations, we do have a subsidy policy. They are themselves responsible for operations.

113876.explanation: The current capacity is insufficient. The complexity of society is increasing and the police are transferring more tasks. Safety is important to us for livability.

113877.explanation: No more than necessary, but only in places where they are genuinely needed.

113882.explanation: The holiday homes are for recreational use and we want to preserve them for that. They are important for our local economy.

113883.explanation: We want to preserve the green character of our municipality, but also see the need to remain flexible due to the high demand for housing.

113884.explanation: This can also be volunteer work.

113885.explanation: We are solidary and look out for others.

113888.explanation: We grow along with the need, but this must not hinder housing development.

113889.explanation: Where the traffic situation requires it, 30 km/h is sensible.

113890.explanation: Of course, only if it is practically feasible.

113892.explanation: It is not a goal in itself. We are below the national average and we want to keep it that way.

113895.explanation: If the possibilities exist and the local entrepreneurs have a need for it.

113896.explanation: We can consider it if the entrepreneurs require it.

113899.explanation: Only allow it when more obvious options have been utilized. First on the roof, then possibly on the land.

113904.explanation: We do not want cluttering of the neighborhood or nuisance for other residents.

113905.explanation: This does not apply to the entire center of Epe, but only to the part of Hoofdstraat between Pastoor Somstraat and Emmastraat and to the Markt between Verbindingsweg and Hoofdstraat.

113906.explanation: This is already existing policy. We are in favor of transparency.

113853.explanation: We are not in favor of referendums. However, we do support citizen participation. Involve residents in a timely manner in plans that are being developed and forthcoming.

113854.explanation: This must certainly be possible, provided it falls within frameworks set by the municipality/government.

113857.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn advocates for local support for resident initiatives and cooperatives.

113860.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn is not in favor of a full-scale asylum seekers center based on 300 people or more. The distribution law gives us little choice. It is therefore important to look at what is appropriate in our municipality. Think of small-scale shelter or cooperation with other surrounding municipalities.

113861.explanation: As far as possible and small-scale without nuisance for neighbors. Our preference is normal housing.

113865.explanation: We are in favor of a ban on consumer fireworks. But we do want to give residents a festive New Year.

113868.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for that.

113869.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn is not in favor of building on agricultural land; the same applies to solar parks and wind turbines.

113871.explanation: From national politics it has been decided that regional broadcasters will operate from regions instead of municipalities. As a result, there will be less 'news' provided from the municipality of Epe. Nieuwe Lijn is not in favor of financially supporting this form of local news provision.

113873.explanation: Unless there is, for example, mismanagement. So financial support within the set frameworks.

113876.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn advocates sufficient capacity, matched to the severity and amount of work.

113877.explanation: We prioritize the safety of residents.

113882.explanation: We are of the opinion that recreational homes are intended for tourists and not for permanent residence by inhabitants.

113883.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn wants to protect the greenery outside the village boundaries as much as possible.

113884.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn believes it is important that appropriate social work is considered in these cases.

113885.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn thinks that residents with a low income and payment problems should be able to discuss this with the municipality of Epe.

113888.explanation: It is an ongoing development to ensure equal coverage throughout the municipality. However, this must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces (in residential neighborhoods).

113889.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn believes that in each situation/street, it should be determined whether 50 km or 30 km per hour is more appropriate.

113890.explanation: The idea is nice but we find it unrealistic to have many different care providers in our municipality for all different groups of people.

113892.explanation: We find it important that municipal charges, such as property tax (OZB), do not increase more than necessary. Especially in times of economic pressure, our residents deserve financial peace and stability. Nieuwe Lijn advocates relief in charges for our residents.

113895.explanation: More businesses in industrial parks are good for employment and the local economy.

113896.explanation: The municipality has a regulation that governs store opening hours, but shopkeepers can adjust their opening times.

113899.explanation: We prioritize solar panels on the roofs of private individuals and businesses first.

113904.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn proposes to investigate this. Is there interest for this in our municipality?

113905.explanation: We want to make both the center of Vaassen and Epe car-free.

113906.explanation: Nieuwe Lijn stands for transparency, but only in exceptional cases do we want to be able to meet behind closed doors.

113853.explanation: A referendum seems attractive, but it is a very expensive method and not suitable for complex issues, as these often require more nuance and considerations than a simple yes/no choice. We believe it is better to involve residents in other ways so that decisions are made carefully.

113854.explanation: If residents take over the management of a sports hall or park, it increases involvement and provides customized solutions that better match local wishes. This gives residents more responsibility and can lead to more efficient and sustainable use of facilities.

113857.explanation: Citizen initiatives to jointly generate sustainable energy are valuable but should in principle be established without structural municipal subsidies. The VVD prefers removing rules and facilitating initiatives over financial support from public funds.

113860.explanation: The VVD believes that a shelter center (AZC) does not automatically fit with Epe and has no preference for one here. At the same time, the VVD recognizes that there is a legal obligation, whereby the municipality must act responsibly and realistically.

113861.explanation: Because this lets entrepreneurs take responsibility and reduces pressure on the housing market. However, this must take place within clear rules to protect housing quality, livability, and the landscape.

113865.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show is not a core task of the municipality, and the money can be better spent on safety and facilities important throughout the year. If there is demand for a show, we prefer initiatives from entrepreneurs, associations, or sponsors.

113868.explanation: Accessible buildings ensure that everyone can participate and strengthen the local economy. Municipal support for entrepreneurs can help here, provided it is purposeful, temporary, and complementary to the entrepreneur's own responsibility.

113869.explanation: By giving farmers space to build homes on their own land, customized solutions can be created that contribute to business succession and additional housing in rural areas. This must be done carefully, with attention to landscape, livability, and clear spatial frameworks.

113871.explanation: The municipality already provides financial support to the local broadcaster, which is sufficient to carry out its public task. Additional municipal funding may jeopardize its independence and objectivity and is therefore undesirable.

113873.explanation: Sports clubs play an important role in health and social cohesion in Epe. If they temporarily encounter problems, targeted and temporary municipal support under conditions can help keep clubs future-proof.

113876.explanation: More visible enforcement officers contribute to safety and livability in neighborhoods and villages in Epe. The current number of enforcement officers is lower than what is deemed appropriate based on common guidelines, so extra effort is needed.

113877.explanation: More camera surveillance is not a goal in itself and must not come at the expense of residents' privacy and freedom. The VVD finds camera surveillance acceptable only as a last resort in places where there are demonstrable and persistent safety issues.

113882.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in detached holiday homes can help reduce housing pressure in Epe.

113883.explanation: To build enough homes, there must be room to develop also outside existing village boundaries. The VVD believes this must be done carefully, with attention to landscape and livability, but does not want to block housing construction by default.

113884.explanation: It is reasonable to expect that people on social assistance perform socially useful work within their abilities. This helps people stay active, increases their chances of finding work (where possible), and contributes to society.

113885.explanation: Exemption from municipal taxes should not be automatic and must not discourage financial independence. At the same time, the VVD values customized solutions so that exemptions remain possible in cases where otherwise problematic debts would arise.

113888.explanation: Further expanding the number of charging points comes at the expense of regular parking spaces and causes an unbalanced ratio. The VVD believes the number of charging points should remain realistic and align with actual demand.

113889.explanation: A general maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas is unnecessary and overlooks differences between roads. The VVD wants lower speeds to be targeted at high-risk locations, such as near primary schools and sports clubs, where traffic safety requires extra attention.

113890.explanation: Choice in healthcare is important and people should feel safe and understood by their care provider. At the same time, this cannot always be guaranteed and quality and availability of care must remain leading.

113892.explanation: A lower property tax keeps living and entrepreneurship affordable in Epe. The VVD believes the municipality should critically review its expenditures before increasing charges for residents and entrepreneurs.

113895.explanation: Expansion of industrial parks is needed to give entrepreneurs space to grow and to maintain employment in Epe. This must be done carefully, considering accessibility, sustainability, and the environment.

113896.explanation: More shopping Sundays give entrepreneurs the freedom to decide when they are open.

113899.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can, alongside roofs and other suitable locations, contribute to the energy transition in Epe. The VVD wants to make this possible within clear spatial frameworks, with attention to landscape, agriculture, and livability.

113904.explanation: Allowing room rentals can help alleviate the housing shortage. However, the municipality must set clear rules to prevent nuisance, unsafe situations, and overcrowding in residential areas.

113905.explanation: A low-traffic center makes the center of Epe safer, more attractive, and more pleasant for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. The VVD chooses for good accessibility and sufficient parking at the edges of the center.

113906.explanation: Non-public meetings may only take place if strictly necessary, for example, to protect privacy or safety interests.

113853.explanation: We support participation and Epe has nice platforms such as 'Epe spreekt' where residents can express their opinions. But we do not support a referendum.

113854.explanation: Management of a sports hall or park must be done (or outsourced) through the municipality. We are in favor of selling small green spaces.

113857.explanation: We support sustainable energy, but currently more focus is needed on the storage of sustainable energy.

113860.explanation: The emergency shelter at the Fletcher hotel closes in March. However, we support maintaining the dispersal law. This means that a (small) asylum seekers' center should be possible in Epe.

113861.explanation: Yes, provided it is a good and reasonable shelter. That would relieve the housing market.

113865.explanation: This seems like a good alternative if the fireworks ban comes into effect.

113868.explanation: We find it important that buildings are accessible to people with disabilities; a small financial subsidy would be a good idea.

113869.explanation: We favor creative solutions for the housing shortage. But agricultural land has a different designation. It would be unfair if farmers were suddenly allowed to build houses on their land because then their land would become much more valuable. Splitting plots of a farmyard must remain possible.

113871.explanation: Yes, a small contribution for a local broadcaster would be appropriate.

113873.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for the finances of a sports club, but clubs and associations can make use of subsidies.

113876.explanation: Yes, it would be good to slightly expand the capacity.

113877.explanation: Yes, because vandalism and theft can be combated this way.

113882.explanation: Holiday homes have a truly different purpose. Still, we ask the municipality to be lenient in distressing situations.

113883.explanation: More houses need to be built, even if that means expanding the village boundary.

113884.explanation: This should be encouraged but can be difficult to make mandatory.

113885.explanation: This should remain possible for people with a low income.

113888.explanation: Many charging points remain unused; expansion does not seem desirable because there are already many spots with limited parking space.

113889.explanation: This would increase the safety of, among others, cyclists. It may not be necessary on every route, but for many routes this would be beneficial.

113890.explanation: Yes, we are therefore happy that 'de Boskamp' can remain in Epe.

113892.explanation: This should remain as it is.

113896.explanation: We believe our society benefits most from a day of rest. Expanding shopping Sundays is not desirable.

113899.explanation: This is not our preference. As a municipal council, we have allowed certain locations. These have not even been realized yet. Let those locations be developed first.

113904.explanation: Strict regulations should be imposed here to combat exploitative landlords. Moreover, we are not a municipality with many students. However, we would support housing splitting where part of a home can be rented out.

113905.explanation: Yes, this would make the center safer and more attractive.

113906.explanation: Municipal council meetings are normally public; that should remain so.

113853.explanation: With a referendum, we give our residents more rights compared to the government and ensure everyone can safely cast their vote. This strengthens democracy.

113854.explanation: The knowledge, skills, and involvement of residents, associations, and businesses can be utilized and increased this way.

113857.explanation: D66 focuses on sustainability for everyone. Financial support should primarily go to residents with limited means.

113860.explanation: After the closure of the emergency shelter in March this year, we must continue fulfilling our duty to accommodate asylum seekers. The dispersal law helps ensure all municipalities contribute.

113861.explanation: Labor migrants deserve dignified housing and a decent wage. Employers and employment agencies bear primary responsibility. The location does not matter.

113865.explanation: D66 is happy with a national fireworks policy that is enforceable. A private initiative for each village center can be permitted when national guidelines exist and there is broad societal support.

113868.explanation: The municipality is mainly responsible for making public buildings and public spaces accessible to people with disabilities. This also includes expanding accessible sidewalks to and from village centers.

113869.explanation: The earning model and future perspective of the agricultural sector should consist of producing healthy food and managing the landscape. For housing construction, farmers are subject to the same rules as other initiators.

113871.explanation: Funding is arranged and standardized nationally. Independent reporting is very valuable; if this is jeopardized, an appeal to the municipality should be possible.

113873.explanation: Sports and physical activity should be a given in our municipality. It keeps us healthy, physically and mentally, and brings people together. When a sports association is struggling, the municipality will assist under conditions.

113876.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) have multiple tasks and are also deployed for prevention. Extra deployment costs extra money. Given the safety risks in our municipality compared to big cities, extra deployment does not seem urgent here.

113877.explanation: Camera surveillance can create a false sense of security and may infringe on privacy.

113882.explanation: We want to investigate whether permanent residence in recreational homes outside holiday parks can be permitted. This allows residents to truly become part of the neighborhood and society and provides perspective for making the home sustainable.

113883.explanation: There is a great need for housing. Infill development is good but can come at the cost of livability. Therefore, expansion adjacent to village edges near main access roads is necessary. Facilities (meeting places, social spaces, green areas) must be secured by the municipality.

113884.explanation: D66 strives to ensure everyone can participate. For people receiving social assistance, 'must' is not appropriate. Encouragement and support are. We do this by advocating for a participation desk.

113885.explanation: We support people with a minimum income in healthcare, sustainability efforts, and reducing burdens. The strongest shoulders should bear the heaviest loads so that everyone can participate.

113889.explanation: This increases the safety of pedestrians and cyclists and discourages shortcut traffic.

113890.explanation: D66 stands for inclusion and diversity. In good care, professionalism is paramount.

113892.explanation: An increase in property tax (OZB) should only be implemented when truly necessary. According to D66, a reduction is not currently on the agenda.

113895.explanation: Expansion of business parks should be aimed at existing companies and new companies that contribute to employment, the local economy, and innovation. Our entrepreneurs have a great need for expansion.

113896.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneurs themselves to reach mutual agreement and gain support. Not the municipality.

113899.explanation: The energy transition requires space. The municipality must ensure good spatial planning so that all necessary functions have a responsible place. Solar panels cannot be placed everywhere, and sufficient agricultural land must remain for food production.

113904.explanation: The number of residents per home has decreased sharply in recent years. Renting out rooms offers more people housing.

113905.explanation: D66 believes that Hoofdstraat should be car-free. This way, the center can become a pleasant place where people see each other more and can meet more easily.

113906.explanation: D66 advocates for an open and transparent way of governing. Our residents and entrepreneurs must know why decisions are made. Therefore, all meetings where decisions are made and prepared should be public.

113853.explanation: Referendums are a controversial tool that can lack nuance and easily lead to an unnuanced majority will, rather than a well-considered weighing of arguments. We prefer citizens' assemblies whose advice is adopted.

113854.explanation: We believe citizen initiatives should be given space and trust. In good consultation with the municipality, residents (groups) should be able to make agreements about management and maintenance of, for example, a sports hall, park, or other facilities.

113857.explanation: We believe collective sustainable initiatives should be supported. Energy cooperatives can play an important role, so we support smooth permits and financial support for such initiatives.

113860.explanation: Epe must loyally meet its obligations under the spread law.

113861.explanation: We believe employers are themselves responsible for adequate housing for seasonal workers. This may also be outside their own business parks.

113865.explanation: A fireworks show is a disproportionate burden on air quality and we see that good alternatives are available. We prefer alternatives such as light shows.

113868.explanation: We find accessible buildings important and also the livability of the centers of our villages. We therefore support dialogue with entrepreneurs about how we can assist them in this. We do not see the necessity to financially support this in all cases.

113869.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, all creative solutions should be possible to reduce this problem, with a focus on maintaining the livability and rural character of our villages.

113871.explanation: We are positive about our local broadcaster, but we also have other challenges in our municipality. If extra funding is needed, we will decide on that later.

113873.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role, but it is not realistic to want to maintain every sports club. Of course, the municipality must make every effort to support sports clubs with advice and assistance.

113876.explanation: Safety is very important to us, but we see no reason to deploy more enforcement officers (BOAs) for this purpose.

113877.explanation: We support targeted and proportional use of cameras, but preferably solve problems in other ways.

113882.explanation: We do not see allowing the use of vacation homes as a permanent residential address as a structural solution. It may possibly be used as a temporary solution.

113883.explanation: We prefer building within the built-up area. In addition, we see sufficient opportunities to build on existing plots in the countryside without harming the living environment.

113884.explanation: We support involving people in socially valuable work, but coercion is not the right way to make people feel valuable to our society.

113885.explanation: We believe that people with debts are helped by forgiveness of municipal taxes. The municipality must focus maximally on preventing people from falling into debt crises.

113888.explanation: We support electric driving, but adding extra parking spaces should only be done when truly necessary. The availability of charging points must be aligned with demand.

113889.explanation: We find the safety of cyclists, children, and elderly important. Most of our roads within built-up areas are already 30 km/h zones. So implementing 30 km/h on all roads within built-up areas is a good idea.

113890.explanation: We believe that free choice of care is important for an inclusive society.

113892.explanation: We do not support a generic reduction of the property tax (OZB).

113895.explanation: We believe there is still sufficient space in existing business parks and therefore see no need for expansion. We want to be attractive to green and sustainable companies and support regional cooperation for the development of business parks.

113896.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs themselves should be able to choose when they are open.

113899.explanation: We support sustainable energy production in our own municipality. We support land-based solar panels if there is double use, for example where the land is used both for (vertical) solar panels and sheep grazing.

113904.explanation: The municipality may and must set conditions, but this solution can also contribute to reducing the housing shortage.

113905.explanation: We are a municipality with a high quality of life. This fits with a car-reduced center where everyone can walk safely and comfortably.

113906.explanation: GL-PvdA wants an open and transparent politics. That means decisions and the discussions about them should in principle be public, so that residents can see what happens and why.

113853.explanation: We find consultation and participation important and valuable for support. However, a referendum should not be a standard instrument. Not every complex issue lends itself to a yes/no choice. Referendums can be complementary, but must not replace careful decision-making.

113854.explanation: We are positive about initiatives where residents take more responsibility for their living environment. This can lead to more involvement and customized solutions. However, it must be sustainable, feasible, and well organized, with clear agreements about finances and responsibilities.

113857.explanation: We appreciate initiatives by residents to jointly generate sustainable energy. The municipality should facilitate and think along but not provide financial support. Sustainable energy is primarily a provincial and national task; municipal resources are intended for local tasks and facilities.

113860.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe believes this issue is mainly determined by national policy. The municipality has limited influence and is bound by national decisions. Epe should only do what is legally required and carefully consider what fits within the capacity of our municipality.

113861.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe believes that the responsibility for housing seasonal workers lies primarily with employers. They make use of these laborers. The municipality can think along and support within the rules, as seasonal workers are important for the local economy.

113865.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe sees this as a difficult trade-off. A fireworks show can increase safety and community spirit, but it also entails costs and organization. Carbid shooting is a local tradition that should continue under clear rules. Customization and consultation are paramount.

113868.explanation: We find building accessibility important, but we see the responsibility primarily with entrepreneurs. This is part of good entrepreneurship and compliance with regulations. Municipal resources are limited; the municipality can inform and think along, but cannot provide structural assistance.

113869.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe believes that building on agricultural land should not be allowed lightly. The rural area is valuable. Under clear conditions, such as parcel or residence splitting, it can sometimes be permitted. Customization and careful spatial consideration are essential in this.

113871.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe sees local media as important for information and democracy. Municipal support can be appropriate, but only with clear agreements about local reporting. If broadcasters merge and are no longer primarily focused on Epe, using public funds is not self-evident.

113873.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe disagrees with this statement as a general rule. Sports clubs are primarily responsible themselves for a healthy financial management. We do not consider structural financial support from the municipality desirable.

113876.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe agrees with this statement. Extra enforcement officers increase visibility and contribute to safety and livability. Public enforcement officers can act faster on nuisance, petty crime, and traffic safety, and strengthen trust in public spaces.

113877.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe disagrees with this statement. We see no necessity for extra camera surveillance as a general measure. Camera surveillance should be targeted and temporary in cases of demonstrable nuisance.

113882.explanation: We do not consider permanent residence in holiday homes self-evident. Tourism is an important source of income for our municipality. Due to housing pressure, customization may be necessary, but in each situation, a careful balance must be struck between living, economy, tourism, and the living environment.

113883.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe disagrees with this statement. The housing shortage sometimes requires building outside existing village boundaries, such as at Zuukerenk. New construction must be carefully integrated with attention to landscape and livability, but excluding it beforehand is unrealistic.

113884.explanation: We are positive about a reciprocal obligation for welfare benefits. Where possible, it is reasonable to give something back to society. However, this must never be mandatory or inhumane: personal situation, health, and capabilities always take precedence. Customization is paramount.

113885.explanation: We disagree with this statement. Debt forgiveness should not be automatic but must remain possible for residents with low incomes who truly need it. A careful consideration per situation is required, with attention to both financial responsibility and humanity.

113888.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe is basically positive about expanding charging options for electric cars. Society has chosen electric driving, and appropriate infrastructure belongs with that.

113889.explanation: We support 30 km/h speed limits within built-up areas where this improves traffic safety and livability. However, this should not be a generic measure. Each street must be assessed to determine what is appropriate and effective, with a clear and safe design of public spaces.

113890.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe believes quality and accessibility of care must always come first. Good care should be available to everyone, regardless of faith, culture, or belief. Personal wishes can be considered but never at the expense of quality, availability, and affordability.

113892.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe disagrees with this statement. Lower charges sound attractive, but municipal services must remain affordable. Reducing the property tax can only be done if financially responsible and fitting within a balanced budget.

113895.explanation: We consider business parks important for employment. Expansion can be considered, but only if there is demonstrable demand and if it fits space, infrastructure, and the living environment. Vacancy must be tackled first and existing sites, such as De Venz, better utilized.

113896.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs should decide for themselves whether Sunday shopping is sensible and profitable. The municipality should not impose it, but should make it possible. Decisions about Sunday opening hours belong to entrepreneurs and residents, not to religious or cultural beliefs. Freedom and choice are paramount.

113899.explanation: We believe solar panels should primarily be installed on roofs of homes, businesses, and existing buildings. Agricultural land is valuable and must not be sacrificed lightly. Only if there are demonstrably no alternatives can this be considered in exceptional cases, with attention to landscape and nature.

113904.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe believes that rooming-in (multiple tenants in one home) should be possible but not unlimited. Renting rooms can help with housing pressure but must not lead to nuisance or harm livability. We choose clear rules and customization: allow where it fits, intervene where necessary.

113905.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Epe is not in favor of a completely car-free center. Important shops and facilities must remain easily accessible. Therefore, we support a phased approach: temporarily make part car-free, evaluate effects, and only then decide on further steps.

113906.explanation: Non-public consultation must never be an end in itself, but at most a temporary means. Decisions and considerations must ultimately always be public. Too strict limitation hinders careful governance and is therefore undesirable.

113853.explanation: Direct democracy is one of FVD's core points.

113854.explanation: Depending on circumstances, the municipality must be open to customization.

113857.explanation: Community funds are not intended for this.

113860.explanation: An asylum seekers’ center has huge consequences for safety and the streetscape. Moreover, it solves nothing. It only leads to even more asylum seekers.

113861.explanation: This causes us to lose control over housing.

113865.explanation: Fireworks should just be left to the people themselves as tradition dictates. If it is banned, the municipality should only enforce against excesses.

113868.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate.

113869.explanation: Within reasonable limits and zoning plans.

113871.explanation: Local broadcasters are important for the local community.

113873.explanation: Sport is important for social cohesion.

113876.explanation: Safety must be enforced by police officers, not by public enforcement officers.

113877.explanation: FVD does not want excessive control. Temporary camera surveillance may be allowed in risk areas.

113882.explanation: Holiday homes are needed for vacations. There must be sufficient construction. FVD wishes everyone a decent home.

113884.explanation: It should not be too attractive to get money from the community for nothing.

113885.explanation: In Epe, arrangements for people with a low income are already very generous. Costs must be fairly distributed among all users.

113888.explanation: This leads to shortages of parking spaces for normal cars. Electrification also causes major problems such as network congestion.

113889.explanation: Epe should not become a slow-motion municipality. Besides, no one adheres to such a low speed.

113890.explanation: Within reason, the wishes of a patient must be taken into account, but there are limits. For example, it should not be allowed for someone to refuse care from a woman due to their religion.

113892.explanation: Municipal charges have risen enormously. It is time to counter this.

113895.explanation: Under certain circumstances, this should be possible. At the same time, space is also needed for other things. It requires an integral consideration. FVD prefers a business park over a solar park, wind turbine, or asylum seekers' center.

113899.explanation: Agricultural land is not meant for this. Solar panels are very polluting to the environment.

113904.explanation: FVD supports adequate housing for everyone.

113905.explanation: Car-free goes too far. Low traffic zones in parts of the center are acceptable.

113906.explanation: FVD has recently taken the initiative towards more transparency. This can be further expanded. Public administration should be open!

113853.explanation: We support a local binding referendum, which can also be organized per village.

113854.explanation: This is already the case and should be allowed.

113857.explanation: No, not mandatory, but for example, the municipality can help people by providing information via the energy cooperative.

113860.explanation: There is already emergency shelter that meets the statutory tasks. We support shelter, but where possible, we prefer small-scale solutions.

113861.explanation: That is allowed but not compulsory. People/workers should have decent housing and not be dependent on their employer for that.

113865.explanation: Of course allowed, but it costs a lot of public money, so we do not want to make this mandatory for the municipality.

113868.explanation: Legal obligation and the municipality must comply so everyone can participate.

113869.explanation: This should be allowed and we have previously supported and helped enable it.

113871.explanation: Yes, agreed. The local broadcaster and press are important to monitor 'power'.

113873.explanation: Yes, sports are important and we gladly support that in our villages.

113876.explanation: Yes, there should be more enforcement.

113877.explanation: Yes, and there should be more enforcement.

113882.explanation: Yes, the municipality should allow holiday homes to be used as permanent residences.

113883.explanation: The municipality should allow new construction outside the existing village boundaries, such as on farms.

113884.explanation: Yes, people receiving social assistance benefits should do socially meaningful work in return.

113885.explanation: Disagree. The municipality should not stop waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes.

113888.explanation: Adapt to what is needed, but don’t overdo it.

113889.explanation: Yes at shopping centers and village cores, but not within the entire built-up area.

113890.explanation: People who need care should be able to choose a care provider who matches their religion, culture, or belief.

113892.explanation: Increase as little as possible, but lowering would come at the expense of investments in our local community (care, roads, sports, culture, and the like).

113895.explanation: In Epe, the business parks may be expanded.

113896.explanation: The municipality should allow Sunday shopping.

113899.explanation: If an owner wants that, then it should be allowed. We are not against it.

113904.explanation: The municipality should also allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.

113905.explanation: We are starting a pilot that we support but want to wait for the results first. If the evaluation is positive, a car-free center of Epe is a serious option.

113906.explanation: Do not hold meetings behind closed doors.

statements.title: The municipal council may only hold secret meetings in exceptional cases.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Epe should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents of Epe should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Epe moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
More camera surveillance should be installed in Epe.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The centre of Epe should become car-free.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: In Epe, the business parks may be expanded.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).
